ai_strategy_lead.name: Marylyn D. Ritchie
ai_strategy_lead.title: Vice Dean of AI and Computing, Perelman School of Medicine; Penn AI Council member

documents.title: Statement on Guidance for the University of Pennsylvania Community on Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence
Provost's Office Announces Penn AI Council

ai_strategy.summary: Penn's AI strategy is coordinated through the Provost's Office via the Penn AI Council (established October 2024) and the Penn AI Initiative (launched 2025). The strategy spans five thematic pillars (AI Foundations, AI+Health, AI+Science, AI+Business, AI+Society) and is underpinned by the Penn Advanced Research Computing Center (PARCC), a school-level vice dean of AI in Perelman, the Wharton AI & Analytics Initiative, and Penn GSE's AI pedagogy work. Policy direction comes from a 2023 university-wide guidance document developed by Information Security, the Privacy Office, and multiple provost offices.

key_provisions: Transparency: disclose when AI tools are used in work product
Accountability: validate accuracy of AI-generated content, guard against hallucinations
No moderate/high-risk Penn data in AI tools without Privacy Office review and contractual protections
Explicit prohibition on exposing patient information (identifiable or deidentified) to public AI tools (HIPAA)
IRB approval required before exposing research participant data to AI
Faculty set course-level AI policies; Code of Academic Integrity applies; treat AI assistance as you would assistance from another person
Schools and departments may issue more specific guidance (Wharton, Law, Medicine have supplemental policies)
ai_policy.summary: Penn's generative AI guidance is detailed, principle-based, and applies to the entire community. It embraces generative AI while enforcing guardrails on data privacy, HIPAA/FERPA, IP, bias, and academic integrity. Faculty retain course-level discretion, with the Center for Excellence in Teaching, Learning, and Innovation (CETLI) providing syllabus and assessment templates.

bodies.name: Penn AI Council
bodies.scope: Five-member senior faculty council convened by the Provost and Senior Vice Provost for Research. Provides strategic input, coordinates cross-disciplinary AI research, engages all 12 schools, and leads planning for the Penn Advanced Research Computing Center. Inaugural members: Duncan Watts, Eric Bradlow, Bhuvnesh Jain, Marylyn Ritchie, Rene Vidal.

bodies.name: Perelman School of Medicine Vice Dean of AI and Computing
bodies.scope: Inaugural vice dean role (Marylyn Ritchie, April 2024) with responsibility for AI strategy across Penn Medicine's research, education, and healthcare delivery missions.

bodies.name: Information Systems & Computing (ISC) AI Guidance Team
bodies.scope: ISC (in collaboration with the University Privacy Office, Office of the Vice Provost for Education, Office of the Vice Provost for Faculty, Senior Vice Provost for Research, Office of Clinical Research, General Counsel, Procurement, and Penn Center of Innovation) authored and maintains the university-wide generative AI guidance and operates enterprise AI tool procurement and security review.

bodies.name: Wharton AI & Analytics Initiative (WAIAI) Leadership
bodies.scope: Wharton-school initiative (Eric Bradlow, Vice Dean of AI and Analytics) coordinating eight specialized labs including Generative AI Labs, Accountable AI Lab, Causal Data Science Lab, Human-AI Research, and Healthcare Analytics Lab.

bodies.name: Penn AI Pedagogy Initiative (PAPI)
bodies.scope: University-wide co-design initiative led by Seiji Isotani (Penn GSE) for AI in teaching and learning, connecting student teams with faculty partners.

governance_structure.summary: Penn operates a multi-layered AI governance structure: the Penn AI Council provides university-wide strategic coordination; a vice dean of AI leads Penn Medicine; Wharton, Law, SEAS, and GSE run their own AI leadership and guidance bodies; ISC provides the cross-cutting policy, privacy, and security floor.

initiatives.title: Penn AI Council established to coordinate university-wide AI strategy
initiatives.description: The Provost's Office and Office of the Vice Provost for Research announced the inaugural Penn AI Council on October 1, 2024, composed of five senior faculty (Duncan Watts, Eric Bradlow, Bhuvnesh Jain, Marylyn Ritchie, Rene Vidal) to act as strategic advisors, coordinate cross-school research, and lead planning for the Penn Advanced Research Computing Center.
initiatives.significance: Formalizes faculty-led AI governance at a decentralized Ivy League university and ties governance directly to compute buildout — a pattern peers are replicating.

investment.description: Faculty effort and Provost's Office operational support; no separate budget disclosed.

collaborations.description: Council explicitly charged with engaging every Penn school.

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates a durable governance body whose remit includes compute, research coordination, and strategic advising to senior leadership.

initiatives.title: University-wide generative AI guidance issued
initiatives.description: In November 2023 Penn published a 'Statement on Guidance for the University of Pennsylvania Community on Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence' — a nine-category framework (transparency, accountability, bias, data protection, HIPAA, IP, academic integrity, research integrity, vendor review) that applies to faculty, staff, students, pre- and post-doctoral trainees.
initiatives.significance: One of the earliest university-wide comprehensive AI guidance documents from an Ivy League institution, setting a template for shared services (ISC + Privacy + Provost) authorship.

investment.description: Staff effort across multiple offices; ongoing operational support for consultations.

partners: Office of the Vice Provost for Education
Office of the Vice Provost for Faculty
collaborations.description: Nine co-authoring offices — a notably broad internal coalition.

initiatives.policy_implications: Sets baseline norms on data classification, HIPAA, IP, and academic integrity that every school can extend but not contradict.

initiatives.title: Penn Advanced Research Computing Center (PARCC) launched with NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD
initiatives.description: Penn partnered with Flexential and NVIDIA to deploy PARCC at Flexential's Philadelphia-Collegeville data center, anchored by an NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD with DGX B200 systems and Quantum-2 InfiniBand. Phase I began in May 2025 and doubles Penn's computing capacity with 7.2 MW of power and 1,500+ watts per square foot with liquid cooling.
initiatives.significance: Major HPC buildout gives Penn Ivy-competitive AI training infrastructure and signals the shift from distributed, faculty-managed clusters to centralized AI compute among R1 privates.

investment.funding_source: University capital; commercial colocation contract with Flexential
investment.description: Specific investment amount not disclosed; hardware includes NVIDIA DGX B200 SuperPOD.

collaborations.description: Flexential provides colocation, NVIDIA hardware and Mission Control software, AHEAD rack integration.

tools_and_vendors.name: DGX SuperPOD with DGX B200 systems

initiatives.policy_implications: Centralized compute gives Penn leverage to attach data governance, security, and fair-access policies to AI research at the infrastructure layer.

initiatives.title: Raj and Neera Singh B.S.E. in Artificial Intelligence — first Ivy League undergraduate AI degree
initiatives.description: Penn Engineering launched the Raj and Neera Singh Program in AI, offering a BSE in AI — the first Ivy League undergraduate AI major and one of the first AI undergraduate engineering programs in the U.S. Students entered the program in fall 2024. Curriculum covers machine learning, algorithms, data analytics, advanced robotics, plus AI safety, ethics, and social good. Program director: George J. Pappas.
initiatives.significance: A flagship credential in the Ivy League that signals AI has moved from concentration/elective into standalone ABET-track engineering degree status — likely to pressure peers.

investment.funding_source: Raj and Neera Singh naming gift to Penn Engineering
investment.description: Gift amount not publicly disclosed; named program + major curriculum investment.

collaborations.description: Internal Penn Engineering program across ESE and CIS.

initiatives.policy_implications: Encodes AI ethics and safety as required components of an accredited engineering degree.

initiatives.title: Cooperative AI advising agreement with the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania
initiatives.description: On October 30, 2025, Penn President J. Larry Jameson and PA Chief Transformation Officer Ben Kirshner signed a cooperative agreement under which Penn provides the Shapiro administration with AI expertise on organizational strategy, risk assessment, and governance — leveraging Penn faculty experts. Announced at the 'Unlocking AI for Public Good' summit.
initiatives.significance: Atypical formalized university-state AI advisory partnership; provides a template for how R1s can monetize or contribute public-interest AI capacity to state government.

investment.description: Cooperative agreement; compensation structure not public.

partners: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, Governor's Office of Administration
collaborations.description: Penn supplies AI strategy and governance expertise to state government.

initiatives.policy_implications: Positions Penn as a de facto AI policy advisor to the state; informs Pennsylvania's Executive Order-level AI governance.

initiatives.title: Keystone AI + Quantum Factory — seven-university Pennsylvania alliance
initiatives.description: In April 2026 Pennsylvania's seven research universities (Penn, Carnegie Mellon, Drexel, Lehigh, Penn State, Temple, Pitt) and the Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center launched the Keystone AI + Quantum Factory, a commonwealth-level collaboration to pool shared compute, coordinate AI and quantum research, and drive commercialization in energy, manufacturing, agriculture, life sciences, and robotics.
initiatives.significance: First time PA's seven R1s have formally aligned on AI+quantum at the commonwealth scale — a possible model for state-anchored university consortia.

investment.funding_source: Commonwealth and university-level commitments; private partners
investment.description: Shared compute infrastructure and workforce programs; specific total not disclosed in early coverage.

initiatives.policy_implications: Aligns state economic development, compute resources, and higher-ed research into a single coordinated platform.

initiatives.title: NSF AIRFoundry — $18M AI-driven RNA BioFoundry
initiatives.description: Penn Engineering, Penn Medicine's Institute for RNA Innovation, and the University of Puerto Rico-Mayagüez lead a six-year, $18M NSF BioFoundry grant (AIRFoundry) integrating AI into RNA design, synthesis, and delivery. Partners include Drexel, Children's Hospital of Philadelphia, and InfiniFluidics.
initiatives.significance: One of five NSF BioFoundries nationwide; positions Penn at the frontier of AI-for-science and democratizes RNA research tools.

investment.funding_source: U.S. National Science Foundation (NSF BioFoundry program)

collaborations.description: Philadelphia startup co-founded by Penn faculty focused on lipid nanoparticle manufacturing.

initiatives.title: Penn GSE PASS program expands with $1M Google.org grant
initiatives.description: Penn GSE's Pioneering AI in School Systems (PASS) program received a $1M Google.org grant (announced October 28, 2025) and in March 2026 expanded to two PA intermediate units, two PA districts, one Delaware district, and one New Jersey district. Directed by Michael Golden, Betty Chandy, and Lara Paparo with faculty support from Bodong Chen, Seiji Isotani, and Shiyan Jiang.
initiatives.significance: Positions Penn GSE as a national model for K-12 AI professional development — connects R1 research university to regional K-12 pipeline.

investment.description: Google.org $1M investment announced October 2025; initial PASS launch supported by Marrazzo Family Foundation.

collaborations.description: Philanthropic arm of Google funding K-12 AI capacity-building.

initiatives.policy_implications: Pairs AI professional development with policy alignment to emerging state AI standards.

initiatives.title: Penn GSE co-leads $26M K-12 AI education grants program
initiatives.description: In February 2026, Penn GSE announced it was co-leading a $26 million grant program to develop and support AI-driven tools for K-12 education infrastructure, including responsible innovation research and practice-oriented field projects.
initiatives.significance: Shifts university role from in-house AI research into a funder/coordinator of the K-12 AI edtech ecosystem, at scale.

investment.funding_source: Partnership-funded grants program (partners not fully enumerated in public coverage)

collaborations.description: Grants flow to K-12 practitioner-led projects.

initiatives.policy_implications: Establishes Penn as a K-12 AI capacity-building hub regionally and nationally.

initiatives.title: Microsoft Copilot Chat enabled for Penn community under enterprise data protections
initiatives.description: Penn ISC negotiated enterprise Microsoft Copilot Chat access for anyone with a Penn (or Penn Medicine) Microsoft 365 account. Users can leverage GPT-class models while protected by Penn's data security agreements with Microsoft. Also available: GitHub Copilot via the GitHub Campus Program, with access to models from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google.
initiatives.significance: Shifts enterprise AI from shadow use to governed, contracted access — a common pattern across R1s but notable for its breadth at Penn.

investment.description: Enterprise Microsoft 365 contract covers Copilot Chat; GitHub Campus program for students.

collaborations.description: Enterprise vendor contracts with data protection terms.

tools_and_vendors.name: GitHub Copilot
tools_and_vendors.role: Developer assistant; includes Claude Sonnet/Opus and Gemini models for premium queries

initiatives.policy_implications: Gives the community a Penn-contracted option so sensitive data does not flow to unreviewed third-party tools.

initiatives.title: Perelman School of Medicine appoints inaugural Vice Dean of AI and Computing
initiatives.description: In March/April 2024 Perelman SOM appointed Marylyn D. Ritchie as inaugural Vice Dean of Artificial Intelligence and Computing. Ritchie also directs the Institute for Biomedical Informatics and is VP for Research Informatics at Penn Medicine.
initiatives.significance: One of the earliest vice-dean-level AI leadership roles at a top medical school — a structural signal for peer academic medical centers.

investment.description: New vice dean role and office support.

collaborations.description: Bridges academic medicine, research informatics, and health-system operations.

initiatives.policy_implications: Formalizes medical-school-level AI strategy with direct authority over informatics and biobank.

initiatives.title: Wharton AI & Analytics Initiative (WAIAI) and Generative AI Labs
initiatives.description: Wharton announced the AI & Analytics Initiative in May 2024 as an umbrella for eight specialized labs: Generative AI Labs (Ethan and Lilach Mollick, co-directors), Accountable AI Lab, Causal Data Science Lab, Forensic Analytics Lab, Healthcare Analytics Lab, Human-AI Research, People Analytics, and Sports Analytics and Business Initiative. Vice Dean: Eric Bradlow.
initiatives.significance: Largest business-school AI investment to date; Mollicks' Gen AI Labs influences corporate AI practice globally, and the umbrella structure is being copied at peer B-schools.

investment.description: Major internal investment; specific dollar figure not publicly disclosed.

collaborations.description: Corporate memberships drive applied AI research and hack-AI-thon.

initiatives.policy_implications: Emphasizes experimentation, transparency, and accountable AI research rather than prescriptive restrictions.

initiatives.title: Penn GSE Penn AI Pedagogy Initiative (PAPI)
initiatives.description: PAPI is a university-wide project led by Seiji Isotani at Penn GSE that pairs interdisciplinary student teams with faculty partners to co-design AI-supported teaching strategies grounded in real classroom needs.
initiatives.significance: Faculty-student co-design is an emerging model for institutional AI pedagogy change; PAPI is among the most visible such efforts at an Ivy.

investment.description: Fellowship/stipend-style support for student-faculty co-design teams.

initiatives.policy_implications: Couples faculty development with structured student input — a governance model beyond policy-by-memo.

initiatives.title: AI Month at Penn (April 2025 and April 2026)
initiatives.description: University-wide programming across April each year focused on human-centered AI, featuring research talks, cross-school dialogues, student showcases, and the Penn AI annual 'Discovering the Future of AI' awards. The first AI Month ran in April 2025.
initiatives.significance: Signature public-facing convening that keeps faculty, students, and staff aligned on Penn's AI narrative and surfaces cross-school research.

investment.funding_source: Office of the Vice Provost for Research; Penn AI Initiative

initiatives.policy_implications: Normalizes sustained AI-literacy conversations across disciplines.

initiatives.title: Penn professors advise the Pennsylvania Commission on AI
initiatives.description: In February 2026, Penn faculty publicly advised on a Pennsylvania commission report on the impact of AI, recommending statewide guardrails and guidance; Penn serves as an expert bench for state-level AI regulation.
initiatives.significance: Further anchors Penn as the commonwealth's academic AI policy voice and translates research expertise into statute-shaping input.
initiatives.policy_implications: Penn's policy recommendations are likely to shape state-level AI requirements on higher education.

research_centers.name: Wharton Generative AI Labs (GAIL)

research_centers.name: Institute for Biomedical Informatics (IBI)

research_centers.focus: Quantum-AI intersection and quantum information research

research_centers.name: Predictive Intelligence Lab (AI4Science)
research_centers.focus: Scientific machine learning and AI for engineering/science

PARCC (NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD B200 + Quantum-2 InfiniBand; Philadelphia-Collegeville colo)

computing_infrastructure.summary: PARCC, launched May 2025 with Flexential and NVIDIA, is Penn's centralized AI/HPC platform. Anchored by an NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD with DGX B200 systems and Quantum-2 InfiniBand networking, the facility is housed at Flexential's Philadelphia-Collegeville data center, provides 7.2 MW of power at 1,500+ watts/sq ft with liquid cooling, and doubles Penn's prior processing capacity. Supports research across all 12 schools.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Enterprise-contracted Microsoft Copilot access as an equity floor
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: By providing all Penn faculty, staff, and students with institutional Copilot Chat under Penn's data-protection contract, Penn reduces dependence on paid consumer subscriptions and narrows the AI tool-access gap within the community.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Penn GSE PASS regional K-12 expansion
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Brings AI professional development and equity-centered practice to intermediate units and school districts across PA, DE, and NJ — including the School District of Philadelphia — to close AI capability gaps in under-resourced K-12 systems.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Bias and data protection provisions in university AI guidance
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Penn's 2023 guidance explicitly calls out bias in training data and mandates consideration of disparate impact when using AI tools.

regulatory_context.summary: Pennsylvania has an active state-level AI policy posture: Gov. Shapiro's 2023 executive order created a Generative AI Governing Board for state government, Pennsylvania adopted an Artificial Intelligence Policy effective January 13, 2026, governing state agency AI use, and the Pennsylvania Department of Education has released an AI endorsement framework for teacher preparation programs. The governor has explicitly left K-12 and higher-ed AI policy to district and institutional leaders. Penn operates as an advisor to the state via the October 2025 cooperative AI advising agreement and Penn faculty serve on the Pennsylvania commission on AI impact. Penn is private and has no public-system AI policy above it.

controversies_and_litigation.summary: ShinyHunters social-engineering breach of Penn's email and donor systems. Hackers invoked FERPA in ransom messaging and released files on February 4, 2026, after Penn refused a $1M ransom. While not strictly an AI controversy, the breach shapes Penn's risk posture on AI tools that touch student or donor data.

Debate within Penn (reported in Daily Pennsylvanian) about whether the university should impose a single AI policy across courses or preserve college-level and faculty-level discretion. Current practice maintains faculty autonomy with the university guidance as a floor.

narrative_summary: The University of Pennsylvania has built one of the most structurally mature AI postures in the Ivy League over 2023–2026. Governance is anchored by the Penn AI Council (October 2024), a five-member faculty body chartered by the Provost to coordinate cross-school strategy and oversee buildout of the Penn Advanced Research Computing Center. This sits above a comprehensive November 2023 generative AI guidance co-authored by nine university offices, and is reinforced at the school level by an inaugural Vice Dean of AI and Computing at Perelman (Marylyn Ritchie), the Wharton AI & Analytics Initiative (WAIAI) launched in May 2024 with eight specialized labs including the Mollicks' Generative AI Labs, and a broad Penn AI Pedagogy Initiative at Penn GSE. Infrastructure matches the governance ambition: PARCC, launched in May 2025 with Flexential and NVIDIA, delivers an NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD (DGX B200) with Quantum-2 InfiniBand housed at a dedicated 7.2-MW colocation facility, doubling Penn's compute capacity. On the academic side, the Raj and Neera Singh BSE in AI — which admitted its first cohort in fall 2024 — is the first Ivy League undergraduate AI major, paired with online MSE and graduate certificate pathways at Penn Engineering and Wharton. Penn is also the commonwealth's de facto AI policy partner: an October 2025 cooperative AI advising agreement with Governor Shapiro's Office of Administration, faculty service on the state AI commission (February 2026), and leadership in the April 2026 Keystone AI + Quantum Factory alongside CMU, Drexel, Lehigh, Penn State, Temple, Pitt, and the Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center. Investment is substantial and diverse: Amy Gutmann Hall ($137M building opened 2024; $25M Stone gift), the Singhs' BSE naming gift, an $18M NSF AIRFoundry grant, a $1M Google.org PASS grant, and a $26M K-12 AI grants program led by Penn GSE. Enterprise tooling is centered on Microsoft Copilot Chat for all affiliates and GitHub Copilot via the Campus Program, with negotiated data-protection contracts that serve as an equity floor. Notable gaps are the absence of a single named Chief AI Officer (coverage is distributed across provost, school, and council roles), no publicly documented student-facing predictive-analytics retention system, and ongoing security risk surfaced by the late-2025 ShinyHunters breach that will likely constrain future AI data deployments.

gaps_acknowledged: No disclosed dollar amount for the Raj and Neera Singh naming gift.
No publicly documented dedicated institution-wide Chief AI Officer title — AI leadership is distributed across Penn AI Council, Perelman Vice Dean of AI, Wharton vice dean, and the provost's office.
No specific cost figure for PARCC; NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD typical GPU count not disclosed publicly.
No confirmed Penn-run AI chatbot or predictive-analytics retention system surfaced in public reporting (only MSEd academic program and enterprise Copilot rollout).
No explicit syllabus-level AI policy requirement — faculty retain discretion under university guidance.
Full dollar total and donor roster for the $26M K-12 AI grants program not fully enumerated in public coverage.
Detailed Penn adoption of Claude for Education (vs. Copilot/GitHub routing to Claude) not confirmed.
